module.title: SAP TM Implementation, Methodology & Best Practices

topic.title: Testing Strategy in SAP TM (Unit, Integration, UAT & Regression)

learningObjectives.title: Understand SAP TM testing scope
learningObjectives.description: Explain validation of planning, execution, settlement, and integration before go-live.

learningObjectives.title: Understand test type sequencing
learningObjectives.description: Describe Unit, SIT, UAT, and Regression testing flow and purpose.

learningObjectives.title: Understand testing ownership
learningObjectives.description: Identify responsibilities across consultants, QA, business users, and project leadership.

sections.title: Testing Strategy in SAP Transportation Management

sections.content: Testing is one of the most critical phases of an SAP TM implementation. It validates that transportation planning, execution, settlement and integration processes work correctly before production deployment. SAP TM projects typically perform multiple testing cycles including Unit Testing, System Integration Testing (SIT), User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and Regression Testing.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Testing verifies that the configured SAP TM solution satisfies both technical requirements and business expectations before Go-Live.

A company configures Freight Order planning in SAP TM. Consultants first perform Unit Testing. Integration Testing validates communication with SAP S/4HANA and SAP EWM. Business users then execute UAT using real transportation scenarios before approving the solution for production deployment.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Execute end-to-end business scenarios during UAT using realistic master data and production-like transportation processes instead of isolated test cases.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational logistics company executed over 1,200 SAP TM test cases covering planning, carrier selection, freight settlement and integration with SAP EWM before Go-Live. The extensive testing resulted in a highly stable production environment.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember the testing sequence: Unit Testing → System Integration Testing (SIT) → User Acceptance Testing (UAT) → Regression Testing → Go-Live.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Successful Unit Testing does not guarantee a successful implementation. Integration and business process validation through SIT and UAT are equally important.

items: SAP TM projects perform Unit, Integration, UAT and Regression Testing.
SIT validates technical integration across systems.
UAT confirms that business requirements are satisfied.
Regression Testing ensures existing functionality remains unaffected.
Comprehensive testing significantly reduces Go-Live risks.
